23 February 1884  [St George Hanover Square](/wiki/St_George_Hanover_Square "St George Hanover Square"), London, England

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Thomas+Bentley%22+director&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Thomas+Bentley%22+director+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Thomas+Bentley%22+director) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Thomas+Bentley%22+director&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2022)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(23 February 1884 – 23 December 1966) was a British film director. He directed 68 films between 1912 and 1941. He directed three films in the early DeForest [Phonofilm](/wiki/Phonofilm "Phonofilm") sound-on-film process, *The Man in the Street* (1926), *The Antidote* (1927), and *Acci-Dental Treatment* (1928).

Pointer, Michael (1996). *Charles Dickens on the Screen*. Lanham, MD: Scarecrow Press. p. 31; [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-8108-2960-6](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-8108-2960-6 "Special:BookSources/0-8108-2960-6").

[Sweet, Matthew](/wiki/Matthew_Sweet_(writer) "Matthew Sweet (writer)") (2005). *Shepperton Babylon*. Faber & Faber. p. 65-67. [ISBN](/wiki/ISBN_(identifier) "ISBN (identifier)") [0-571-21297-2](/wiki/Special:BookSources/0-571-21297-2 "Special:BookSources/0-571-21297-2").

[*Shepperton Babylon*](https://web.archive.org/web/20231101130637/https://genome.ch.bbc.co.uk/729b79e0de25427da68f6a9fc306d011) (documentary). [BBC Four](/wiki/BBC_Four "BBC Four"). 2005. Archived from [the original](https://genome.ch.bbc.co.uk/729b79e0de25427da68f6a9fc306d011) on 1 November 2023.

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British film director (1884–1966)

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
| [icon](/wiki/File:Question_book-new.svg) | This article **needs additional citations for [verification](/wiki/Wikipedia:Verifiability "Wikipedia:Verifiability")**. Please help [improve this article](/wiki/Special:EditPage/Thomas_Bentley_(director) "Special:EditPage/Thomas Bentley (director)") by [adding citations to reliable sources](/wiki/Help:Referencing_for_beginners "Help:Referencing for beginners"). Unsourced material may be challenged and removed. *Find sources:* ["Thomas Bentley" director](https://www.google.com/search?as_eq=wikipedia&q=%22Thomas+Bentley%22+director) – [news](https://www.google.com/search?tbm=nws&q=%22Thomas+Bentley%22+director+-wikipedia&tbs=ar:1) **·** [newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Thomas+Bentley%22+director&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Thomas+Bentley%22+director+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Thomas+Bentley%22+director) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Thomas+Bentley%22+director&acc=on&wc=on) *(February 2022)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

| Thomas Bentley | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | 23 February 1884  [St George Hanover Square](/wiki/St_George_Hanover_Square "St George Hanover Square"), London, England |
| Died | 23 December 1966 (aged 82)  [Bournemouth](/wiki/Bournemouth "Bournemouth"), [Dorset](/wiki/Dorset "Dorset"), England |
| Occupation | [Film director](/wiki/Film_director "Film director") |
| Years active | 1912–1951 |

**Thomas Bentley** (23 February 1884 – 23 December 1966) was a British film director. He directed 68 films between 1912 and 1941. He directed three films in the early DeForest [Phonofilm](/wiki/Phonofilm "Phonofilm") sound-on-film process, *The Man in the Street* (1926), *The Antidote* (1927), and *Acci-Dental Treatment* (1928).

Bentley was born in [St George Hanover Square](/wiki/St_George_Hanover_Square "St George Hanover Square"), [London](/wiki/London "London") and originally trained as an engineer but went on to become a [vaudeville](/wiki/Vaudeville "Vaudeville") performer well known for impersonating the characters from the [novels](/wiki/Novel "Novel") of [Charles Dickens](/wiki/Charles_Dickens "Charles Dickens") on stage, touring Britain and Australia.[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-Film-2) His directing career in silent films began in 1910 after he was signed by [Cecil Hepworth](/wiki/Cecil_Hepworth "Cecil Hepworth") to write and direct five adaptations of Dickens' novels.[[2]](#cite_note-Film-2) He would go on to direct more Dickens adaptations throughout his career. After his retirement from directing in 1941 he became technical advisor to the British Film Council.

In her typescript-cum-memoir, [Mabel Poulton](/wiki/Mabel_Poulton "Mabel Poulton") named Bentley as the film director-rapist of a young British starlet who then becomes an alcoholic as a result.[[3]](#cite_note-3)[[4]](#cite_note-4) Poulton starred in two films directed by him: *[The Old Curiosity Shop](/wiki/The_Old_Curiosity_Shop_(1921_film) "The Old Curiosity Shop (1921 film)")* (1921) and *[Not Quite a Lady](/wiki/Not_Quite_a_Lady "Not Quite a Lady")* (1928).

## Legacy

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Thomas_Bentley_(director)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Legacy")]

Bentley directed nearly 60 films and many shorts during his career. Despite Bentley's long tenure in the British film industry, his work remains comparatively neglected within academic film circles.[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5) His modern reputation is also viewed unfavorably, with film historian [Rachael Low](/wiki/Rachael_Low "Rachael Low") describing Bentley as a "hum-drum director".[[5]](#cite_note-:0-5)
